title: euodia barbigera
intro: Euodia barbigera, also known as the Japanese pepper or Japanese pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. Native to East Asia, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering relief from symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and coughs. Euodia barbigera can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to address a range of health concerns.
